content.title: First Read Rule
body: If this is the agent's first time reading this README for the current work mission, read the entire README before opening deeper docs, following local routes, or relying on this corpus.

A first full pass is required once per current work mission, after a context reset, after a handoff or resume, or after the task materially changes. It is not required at every checkpoint in the same active mission.

After the first full pass, use the routing sections below to decide which local docs to reread or inspect next. At later checkpoints, reread only route-relevant sections unless the active `.core.*` route, roadmap, audit, or local stop condition requires a broader reread.
Do not treat this README as authority over App Behavior, architecture, implementation plans, proof, source, repo facts, product decisions, or explicit user instruction.
Living Document
Purpose: Index for machine-readable JSON Schema files used by the App Behavior spec system.
Audience: Agents and engineers validating UX JSON files, examples, generators, or DB import/export tooling.
Keep In Sync With: Model docs, examples, and any future validation scripts.
Update When: Required fields, enum values, schema versions, or shared definitions change.
Does Not Cover: Human-readable schema rationale beyond short orientation.

content.title: Schemas
body: This folder contains JSON Schema files for the App Behavior spec system. The schemas define v1 structure while allowing controlled extension through optional fields and `custom` objects.

Use the package manifest schema before authoring detailed items. The manifest declares the profile, depth target, UX modes, required facets, allowed omissions, and completion standard for the package.
`shared-definitions.schema.json` owns common vocabulary such as `mode`, `artifactRole`, `specProfile`, `depthTarget`, `facet`, and the future-consolidation `baseUxItem` definition.
Files are prefixed in recommended reading order.
1. [Shared Definitions](shared-definitions.schema.json)
2. [UX Artifact Envelope](ux-artifact-envelope.schema.json)
3. [UX Spec Package Manifest](ux-spec-package-manifest.schema.json)
4. [UX Coverage Index](ux-coverage-index.schema.json)
5. [UX Feature Item](ux-feature-item.schema.json)
6. [UX Subfeature Item](ux-subfeature-item.schema.json)
7. [UX Journey Item](ux-journey-item.schema.json)
8. [UX Flow Item](ux-flow-item.schema.json)
9. [UX Subflow Item](ux-subflow-item.schema.json)
10. [UX Requirement Item](ux-requirement-item.schema.json)
11. [UX Observable Scenario Item](ux-runtime-scenario-item.schema.json)
12. [UX Acceptance Criterion Item](ux-acceptance-criterion-item.schema.json)
13. [UX Pending Decision Item](ux-pending-decision-item.schema.json)
14. [UX Current Desired Mapping](ux-current-desired-mapping.schema.json)

- `ux-behavior-mapping.schema.json` defines compatibility and cross-row behavior relationship edges; row-local behaviorRelations in shared-definitions.schema.json are preferred for normal App Behavior rows.

- `ux-runtime-drift-item.schema.json` defines compatibility and cross-row UX/runtime drift entries; row-local runtimeAlignment in shared-definitions.schema.json is preferred for normal App Behavior rows.

- `ux-behavior-thread.schema.json` defines parent-container behavior threads. A thread physically contains child rows in behavior, relationships, issues, and context sections; child rows remain the behavior/source truth.

content.title: Routing
body: Use this area when validating, interpreting, or updating UX-system schema-shaped artifacts.
- For general schema interpretation, read the schema docs listed here before judging generated or stored artifacts.
- For confirmation baseline schema questions, read the confirmation baseline schema section before comparing route or behavior outputs.
- Use `.core.behavior`, `.core.ux`, or `.core.docs` when the schema question affects App Behavior authoring, UX modeling, or generated documentation.
Does not own product behavior truth; schema compliance shapes how behavior is recorded, not what behavior should be.
Stop when the agent can state the schema being applied, the artifact being checked, the required fields or shape, and the source authority for the behavior content.

content.title: Confirmation Baseline Schema
body: - `shared-definitions.schema.json#/$defs/confirmationBaseline` defines the common row-level commit baseline shape.
- UX item schemas and coverage rows require the field so scripts can find rows that need audit refresh.
- Use `tools/backfill-confirmation-baseline.cjs` for one-off migration or baseline refresh passes.
